metrics.title: DAU
metrics.why: Baseline health check; a >10% day-over-day drop warrants immediate incident review.

metrics.title: Trailing WAU
metrics.why: 7-day rolling uniques; removes weekday noise better than calendar WAU for trend calls.

metrics.title: Trailing MAU
metrics.why: 28-day rolling base; shows sustainable growth earlier than calendar MAU resets.

metrics.title: Total Online Time
metrics.why: Session volume aggregate; flat despite DAU growth reveals thinning per-user engagement.

metrics.title: Avg Session Length
metrics.why: Per-visit depth signal; sustained decline anticipates DAU churn by 1–2 weeks.

metrics.title: Matchmaking Success Rate
metrics.why: Queue UX health; below threshold means players abandon before a game starts.

metrics.title: Match Drop Rate
metrics.why: In-match quit signal; rising rate flags lag spikes, toxicity, or balance problems.

metrics.title: Avg Queue Wait
metrics.why: Long waits reduce daily match frequency and directly damage session-count retention.

questions.text: DAU by country over the last 30 days — geographic player spread
Daily active players by OS platform — iOS vs Android split
Trailing MAU over the last 60 days — is the player base growing?
Tutorial completion rate by step — where do new players drop off?
First game mode chosen in training — what draws new players?
Backflow logins vs total logins by app channel — returning vs fresh players
Avg ship-training completion time last 30 days — onboarding speed
Newbie self-esteem distribution last 30 days — confident vs anxious starters
Login volume by app channel last 30 days — channel traffic share
Backflow logins by server last 30 days — which servers attract returnees?
Login count by login channel last 30 days — auth method breakdown

metrics.title: Tutorial Completion Rate
metrics.why: Core onboarding KPI; low rate signals FTUE friction before players can activate.

metrics.title: Tutorial Starts
metrics.why: Raw new-player entry volume; sustained drop is the earliest acquisition slowdown signal.

metrics.title: Tutorial Completions
metrics.why: Activated-player count; sets the upper bound for revenue-eligible new cohorts.

metrics.title: Backflow Logins
metrics.why: Win-back volume; a lift following a campaign confirms re-engagement channel effectiveness.

metrics.title: Avg Onboarding Duration
metrics.why: Friction indicator; longer times correlate with early drop-off before first real match.

metrics.title: Avg Newbie Tutorial Score
metrics.why: Quality signal; consistently low scores flag confusing mechanics or weak tutorial design.

metrics.title: Tutorials Touched per Player
metrics.why: Breadth signal; measures how much of the onboarding surface new players actually explore.

metrics.title: Distinct Login Devices
metrics.why: Multi-device reach; growing count indicates successful cross-platform acquisition spread.

questions.text: Payer count per day — active players who recharged, last 30 days
Payer share by OS platform — iOS vs Android recharge rate
Recharge player density by server — top servers by payer share
Diamond spend by gacha banner — which lottery box captures the most?

Ten-pull vs single-pull ratio by gacha banner

Payer VIP level distribution in the most recent month of data
Gold outflow by spend reason in the most recent month of data
Prop gain events vs loss events by quality last 30 days

metrics.title: Total Gacha Diamond Spend
metrics.why: Primary soft-currency sink; tracks banner draw intensity and relative revenue contribution.

metrics.title: Gacha Participating Players
metrics.why: Banner reach metric; low share of DAU signals weak banner appeal or a price barrier.

metrics.title: Total Prop Consumption
metrics.why: Item economy sink depth; validates that game-loop rewards are converting into spend.

metrics.title: Total Currency Outflow
metrics.why: Aggregate spend pressure; compare to inflow ratio to detect inflationary imbalance.

metrics.title: Total Currency Inflow
metrics.why: Reward distribution scale; persistent excess over outflow signals economy inflation risk.

metrics.title: Ten-Pull Count
metrics.why: Bulk commitment gauge; rising ratio to single pulls indicates higher payer conviction.

metrics.title: Diamond-Fueled Pulls
metrics.why: Premium engagement proxy; separates paying players from free-currency gacha activity.

metrics.title: Net Weapon-Point Flow
metrics.why: Weapon economy health; persistent net drain without replenishment risks player frustration.

questions.text: Which whale-tier players haven't logged in for 7+ days — ranked by lifetime spend for win-back prioritization?
What share of MAU are new vs returning vs reactivated players each month?
How has DAU trended over the last 30 days?
Which character classes account for the largest share of distinct active roles in the most recent available data?
How are role classes distributed across each server — which character class dominates which server?
Which role classes are newly joining players choosing month-over-month — is there a shift in class popularity among recently registered characters?

Player base lifecycle stage breakdown — active vs dormant vs churned share
Engagement segment distribution — hardcore, casual, and at-risk player share

metrics.title: Daily Active Users (DAU)
metrics.why: Core engagement health pulse; a sustained drop signals crisis before MAU catches it.

metrics.title: Monthly Active Users (MAU)
metrics.why: Defines addressable audience size; denominator for paying rate and engagement depth ratios.

metrics.title: Total Daily Online Time
metrics.why: Aggregate playtime signal; declining total flags disengagement before DAU shows a drop.

metrics.title: Avg Session Duration
metrics.why: Depth-per-session metric; short averages surface content fatigue or progression blocks early.

metrics.title: Total Login Events
metrics.why: Volume of all login actions; spikes map to event or campaign pull, flatness signals missing trigger.

metrics.title: Active Server Count
metrics.why: Server health indicator; shrinking count signals player migration or content staleness on old servers.

metrics.title: Monthly Active Days (Total)
metrics.why: Aggregate playtime proxy; divide by MAU for avg days per user to track engagement depth trend.

metrics.title: Monthly Recharging Active Users
metrics.why: Active users who also paid this month; declining ratio reveals a monetization-engagement gap.

questions.text: Which monthly install cohorts show the highest lifetime revenue per user — and how does paying rate vary across cohorts?
Rank media sources by new user volume and 30-day paying rate — which acquisition channel brings the highest-value players?
How does lifetime value and paying rate compare between paid-install and organic users?
Which character class do new players pick most often as their first role — and does the choice vary by server?
Which servers generated the most revenue from characters created in the last 90 days — a quality signal for server-level acquisition performance?
Among characters created in the last 60 days, what is the VIP level distribution — how quickly are new players progressing to paid tiers?
Fraud install rate by media source — which channels deliver the cleanest traffic?
New install volume by OS platform — iOS vs Android acquisition split
Top countries by new install count in the most recent month
Which acquisition campaigns have the highest lifetime ARPU?
Paid-install vs organic split of new user volume by month
Which login channels do paid-install users favor — channel preference by install type?

metrics.title: New Role Registrations
metrics.why: In-game character creation volume; leading indicator of top-of-funnel UA campaign output per server.

metrics.title: Approx Total User Count
metrics.why: Fast HLL estimate of total player base; baseline for acquisition funnel sizing and growth tracking.

metrics.title: 30-Day Paying Rate
metrics.why: Recent conversion health metric; compare across channels and cohorts to rank traffic quality.

metrics.title: Lifetime ARPU (VND)
metrics.why: Blended revenue per all users; divide by UA CPI for channel-level ROAS benchmarking.

metrics.title: Total Lifetime Revenue (VND)
metrics.why: Absolute revenue contribution of the acquired base; compare cohorts to validate UA payback periods.

metrics.title: 30-Day LTV (VND)
metrics.why: Near-term return on UA spend; benchmark new cohorts against targets to catch underperformers early.

metrics.title: Total Paying Users
metrics.why: Absolute lifetime payer count; combined with install volume yields overall conversion funnel rate.

metrics.title: Distinct Registered Users
metrics.why: Unique SDK accounts with at least one character; separates account creation from character funnel.

questions.text: What share of total lifetime revenue comes from whales vs dolphins vs minnows — and how many players are in each tier?
List top VIP players ranked by VIP level and lifetime spend for high-touch CS outreach
How has monthly ARPPU trended over the most recent months of data, and how many paying users drove it?
Which character classes generate the most total revenue — and which have the highest revenue per active player?
Which character classes climb the VIP ladder fastest — VIP level distribution broken down by role class?
Which game servers generate the highest total player spending in the most recent available data?
Revenue split: IAP vs Web payment channel in the most recent month
Daily recharge revenue trend — recent acceleration or decline?
Transaction size distribution — share of small vs large recharge amounts
Top countries by total recharge revenue in the most recent month
Which in-game purchase packages generate the most revenue?
Recharge frequency distribution — how many days per month do payers typically recharge?

metrics.title: Total Recharge Revenue
metrics.why: Primary top-line metric from raw events; always filter currency_code for clean same-currency sums.

metrics.title: Paying Users (Transaction Events)
metrics.why: HLL payer count from event stream; track daily for real-time campaign and promotion impact signals.

metrics.title: ARPPU (Recharge Events)
metrics.why: Revenue per paying user from raw transactions; rising with flat payer count signals higher per-head spend.

metrics.title: Avg Revenue Per Transaction
metrics.why: Average purchase size; guides package pricing decisions and flags which product_ids punch above weight.

metrics.title: Transaction Count
metrics.why: Purchase event volume; spikes map to events and promos, enabling event ROI attribution.

metrics.title: Whale Count
metrics.why: Number of top-value users; an early decline signals VIP churn risk before top-line revenue reacts.

metrics.title: Overall Paying Rate
metrics.why: Share of total player base who have ever paid; structural monetization health benchmark across cohorts.

metrics.title: Monthly ARPPU (VND)
metrics.why: Monthly revenue per paying user from snapshot cube; track MoM to measure event and offer lift.

questions.text: Which whale-tier players haven't logged in for 7+ days and should be prioritized for win-back outreach?
How many paying users are currently flagged as churn-risk, broken down by payer tier?
Which role class logs the most total online time per day over the last 30 days?
How many players have been retained vs recalled as active users in the most recent data — which group is larger?

Which character class logs the most recharge transactions — how does spending activity differ by class?
DAU trend over the last 7 days — is the daily player count rising or falling?
Current WAU/MAU stickiness ratio — how engaged is the active player base?
User count by lifecycle stage — how many are active, at-risk, lapsed, or dormant?

Daily active users by country — which markets drive the most engagement?
Total online time by role-level bracket — do high-level players log the most hours?

metrics.title: DAU
metrics.why: Flags player-count shifts before they become trends; primary daily health pulse for ops decisions

metrics.title: WAU
metrics.why: Mid-window engagement signal; leads MAU changes by days, giving earlier warning of churn waves

metrics.title: MAU
metrics.why: Monthly player breadth; denominator for WAU/MAU stickiness ratio and retention benchmarking

metrics.title: Trailing WAU
metrics.why: Running week-to-date actives through today; real-time engagement pacing against prior week

metrics.title: Total Online Time
metrics.why: Aggregate playtime; drops signal content fatigue or broken sessions before DAU moves

metrics.title: Lapsed Users (MTD)
metrics.why: Early-month lapse count; informs timing and audience for re-engagement push and email campaigns

metrics.title: Whale Count
metrics.why: Count of top-tier payers; sudden drops predict near-term revenue loss before it hits totals

metrics.title: Active Servers
metrics.why: Live server count; ecosystem health indicator and input for server consolidation decisions

ROAS by media source — which acquisition channel generates the best return on ad spend?

Payer conversion rate by platform — iOS or Android turns more new users into payers?

metrics.title: Installs
metrics.why: Top-of-funnel volume; baseline denominator for all downstream conversion and cost metrics

metrics.title: NRU (New Registered Users)
metrics.why: Install-to-registration conversion; quality filter distinguishing real UA from incentivised noise

metrics.title: CPI (VND)
metrics.why: Cost-per-install; primary lever for budget reallocation and campaign-bid optimisation

metrics.title: ROAS
metrics.why: Revenue returned per ad dollar; determines which channels to scale, hold, or cut

metrics.title: NNPU (Net-New Paying Users)
metrics.why: First-ever payers attributed per channel; measures a channel's ability to seed the revenue funnel

metrics.title: D1 Retention
metrics.why: Day-1 return rate; strongest leading predictor of D30 retention and long-term lifetime value

metrics.title: D7 Retention
metrics.why: Week-1 cohort quality; tracks habit formation before the main churn window closes

metrics.title: Marketing Spend (VND)
metrics.why: Total ad investment; budget pacing against monthly plan and ROAS floor thresholds

questions.text: Which in-game products generate the most revenue and transaction volume in the most recent data?
How does average lifetime revenue per paying user compare across monthly install cohorts over the last 6 months?
Compare iOS vs Android on ARPPU and paying-user conversion rate — which platform monetizes better?
Which payment channels generate the most total recharge revenue — rank telco, IAP, web, and other channels by VND?
Which in-game products drive the most recharge revenue — rank all purchasable items by total VND collected?
Which character class has the highest average revenue per paying user — do certain classes attract bigger spenders?

metrics.title: Total Revenue
metrics.why: Daily headline P&L signal; benchmarks live-ops event impact and surfaces revenue anomalies fast

metrics.title: ARPPU (VND)
metrics.why: Average revenue per paying user; depth-of-spend benchmark across channels, classes, and servers

metrics.title: Daily Paying Users
metrics.why: Unique payers per day; tracks paying-cohort engagement independently of spend magnitude

metrics.title: Trailing MPU
metrics.why: Month-to-date unique payers; real-time pacing gauge against monthly monetisation targets

metrics.title: Total LTV (VND)
metrics.why: Aggregate lifetime value across all users; sizes the monetisable installed base

metrics.title: IAP Revenue
metrics.why: App-store revenue stream; isolates platform-fee exposure and store-policy risk from web spend

metrics.title: Payer Rate (NRU→NPU)
metrics.why: New-user-to-payer conversion rate; top efficiency metric of the monetisation funnel per cohort

metrics.title: Lifetime Paying Rate
metrics.why: All-time payer share of total user base; stable benchmark for overall monetisation health

metrics.title: Daily Active Users (DAU)
metrics.why: Core engagement pulse; gates server-merge decisions and daily event targeting windows

metrics.title: Weekly Active Users (WAU)
metrics.why: Measures week-level lift from live events and monitors decay between events

metrics.title: Monthly Active Users (MAU)
metrics.why: Baseline health benchmark; month-over-month delta reveals structural growth or erosion

metrics.title: Trailing WAU (week-to-date)
metrics.why: Real-time weekly active count; informs mid-week event adjustments before ISO week closes

metrics.title: Trailing MAU (month-to-date)
metrics.why: Month-in-progress active base; enables early forecast vs. prior month final before month closes

metrics.title: Total Online Time (seconds)
metrics.why: Proxy for content depth; declining aggregate session time signals disengagement before DAU itself drops

metrics.title: Lapsed Users This Month
metrics.why: Sizes the re-engagement campaign pool; users active last month but absent this month are the highest-probability win-backs

metrics.title: Active Server Count
metrics.why: Server population health indicator; sustained decline signals need for server merges to preserve community density

questions.text: Rank acquisition channels by ROAS this month — which channel delivers the best return on ad spend?
Plot the D1 → D30 retention curve for the most recent monthly install cohort.
Which acquisition media sources deliver the highest ROAS over the last 30 days of available data — where should we double spend?
Rank acquisition channels by CPI and install volume over the last 30 days of available data — where are we overpaying per install?
Plot the D1 → D30 retention curve for the most recent monthly new-player cohort.
Compare D1 / D7 / D30 retention rates across the last three monthly install cohorts — is retention improving?
NRU trend by week over the last 3 months — is the new-player pipeline growing?
Paid vs organic NRU split this month — what share of new users are bought?
D1/D7/D30 retention for paid vs organic installs — do bought users stick?
Top campaigns by NRU this month — which creatives drive the most registrations?
Payer rate by media source this month — which channels bring the best quality users?
Total ad spend by media source this month — where is the budget concentrated?

metrics.title: New Registered Users (NRU)
metrics.why: Top-of-funnel volume; all downstream UA metrics (retention, payer conversion, LTV) are denominated on NRU

metrics.title: Acquisition Spend (VND)
metrics.why: Budget tracker per channel; pairs with NRU and installs to compute cost-per-registration and flag overspend

metrics.title: Cost Per Install (CPI, VND)
metrics.why: Primary channel efficiency metric; drives reallocation of budget from high-CPI to low-CPI sources

metrics.title: Return on Ad Spend (ROAS)
metrics.why: Revenue-to-spend ratio; single best signal for scaling a channel — ROAS > 1 means the channel is self-funding

metrics.title: Payer Rate (NPU / NRU)
metrics.why: Acquisition quality proxy; high payer rate identifies channels that deliver buyers, not just downloaders

metrics.title: Day-1 Retention
metrics.why: Onboarding effectiveness signal; D1 below benchmark indicates first-session UX friction before users form a habit

metrics.title: Day-7 Retention
metrics.why: One-week survival rate; strong leading indicator of D30 LTV and used as a campaign quality gate

metrics.title: ARPPU — New Cohort (VND)
metrics.why: Revenue per new paying user; multiplied by payer_rate yields a fast cohort LTV estimate for bid-cap decisions

questions.text: How is total lifetime revenue distributed across whale, dolphin, and minnow payer tiers — what share do whales contribute?
Rank the top VIP players by VIP level and lifetime spend for prioritized customer-success outreach.
What are the D1 / D7 / D30 payer conversion rates for recent install cohorts — at which day do we lose the most potential spenders?
Compare total revenue, paying user count, and ARPPU between iOS and Android over the last 30 days of available data.
Which acquisition media source produces users with the highest average lifetime value and paying rate?
Which role class has the highest ARPPU and paying conversion rate — do certain classes attract bigger spenders?
Revenue by payment channel (IAP vs web) over the last 30 days — channel mix trend
Daily revenue trend over the last 30 days — is total spend accelerating?
ARPPU this month vs last month — is average spend per payer growing?
Top products by revenue this month — which items drive the most spend?
First-purchase volume and revenue by day — how fast are new payers converting?
Transaction size mix this month — micro vs large spend share by band?

metrics.title: Total Daily Revenue (VND)
metrics.why: Top-line health pulse; tracks P&L daily and measures gross ROI of live events and promotions

metrics.title: ARPPU — Raw Transactions (VND)
metrics.why: Spending depth per active payer; rising ARPPU with flat payer count signals healthy monetization of existing base

metrics.title: Daily Paying Users
metrics.why: Revenue base size; dividing daily revenue by this gives daily ARPPU for tracking monetization efficiency over time

metrics.title: Lifetime LTV — All Users (VND)
metrics.why: Aggregate lifetime spend across the full user portfolio; informs maximum sustainable UA cost per install

metrics.title: Whale Player Count
metrics.why: Top-payer segment size; whales often drive 60–80% of revenue — weekly tracking catches early whale churn before revenue drops

metrics.title: New Paying Users (NPU)
metrics.why: Daily payer acquisition count; measures how efficiently each day's new cohort converts to first revenue

metrics.title: Trailing Monthly Paying Users (MPU)
metrics.why: Month-to-date distinct payer count; early-month signal for whether the revenue base is growing vs. the prior month

metrics.title: Lifetime Paying Rate
metrics.why: Share of all registered users who ever recharged; rising rate confirms the onboarding-to-purchase funnel is improving
